title: Flexitarian Diet Weekly Macros Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Flexitarian Diet Weekly Macros & Meal Plan Calculator
description: Flexitarian diet macro calculator. 20% protein, 30% fat, 50% carbs. 5 plant-based + 2 meat days. Free meal planning & carbon footprint tracking.
intro: The **flexitarian diet** (coined by Dawn Jackson Blatner, 2009) is essentially vegetarian with strategic exceptions: 5 plant-based days per week, 2 days with fish or poultry. Standard macro distribution: **20% protein / 30% fat / 50% carbohydrates**. Ranked #2 in US News Best Diets and #1 in sustainable diets, it delivers cardiovascular benefits, sustainable weight loss, and a 25% reduction in environmental footprint—without the strictness of veganism. This calculator shows your daily macro targets and how to distribute animal and plant-based protein across the 7-day week. Perfect for people who want to reduce meat consumption without eliminating it entirely.
keyTakeaway: Flexitarian: **20% protein + 30% fat + 50% carbs**. 5 plant-based days + 2 with meat/fish. -15% heart disease mortality, -25% carbon footprint.
useCases: Reduce meat consumption without going vegetarian

example.title: Example: 2000 calories flexitarian
steps: **Weekly plan**: 5 plant-based days + 2 days with meat/fish.
example.result: Plant-based days: legumes + tofu + eggs. Meat days: 150g protein source. Carbon footprint: -25%.

explanation:
## What is the flexitarian diet?

**Flexible + vegetarian**. Plant-based is your default.

## Three flexitarian levels (Blatner)

| Level | Meat/week |
|---|---|
| Beginner | 6–8 servings |
| Advanced | 3–4 servings |
| Expert | 0–2 servings |

## Macro targets

- 20% protein (plant and animal sources).
- 30% fat (olive oil, avocado, fatty fish).
- 50% whole-grain carbs.

## Sample 7-day meal structure

| Day | Protein source |
|---|---|
| Monday | Legumes (beans, lentils) |
| Tuesday | Tofu or tempeh |
| Wednesday | Fish or salmon |
| Thursday | Legumes + eggs |
| Friday | Poultry (chicken, turkey) |
| Saturday | Legumes |
| Sunday | Eggs |

## Health benefits

- **-15% cardiovascular mortality**.
- **-20% type 2 diabetes risk**.
- **-25% carbon footprint**.
- Typical weight loss: 4–6 kg over 6 months.

## Common mistakes

1. **Ultra-processed "veggie" options**: Just because it's plant-based doesn't mean it's healthy. Avoid processed meat alternatives and focus on whole foods.
2. **Inadequate protein intake**: Aim for 1.6–2.2g per kg of body weight daily.
3. **Not counting fish correctly**: Fish and seafood count toward your 2 allowed days—track them.

## Related calculators

- [Vegan diet macros](/calculadora-macros-vegana-dieta).
- [Mediterranean diet macros](/calculadora-macros-mediterranea).
- [Daily protein requirements](/calculadora-proteina-diaria-objetivo).

Consult a registered dietitian for personalized guidance.

faq.a: A mostly plant-based diet with strategic meat or fish 2 days per week. 20% protein, 30% fat, 50% carbs. It's flexible vegetarianism—you're not giving up meat entirely.

faq.q: How much meat can I eat on flexitarian?
faq.a: Beginners: 6–8 servings/week (100g per serving). Advanced: 3–4 servings. Experts: 0–2 servings. This flexibility is why it's called flexitarian.

faq.q: Can I eat fish and shellfish on flexitarian?
faq.a: Yes, absolutely. Fish and seafood are encouraged on your 2 non-vegetarian days, typically 2–3 times per week. They're great protein sources and provide omega-3 fats.

faq.q: Is flexitarian diet the same as pescatarian?
faq.a: No. Pescatarian means no meat but fish is okay. Flexitarian means mostly plant-based with occasional poultry, beef, and fish—more flexibility.

faq.q: How much weight can I lose on flexitarian diet?
faq.a: Average weight loss: 4–6 kg (9–13 lbs) over 6 months, depending on calorie intake, exercise, and existing diet patterns.

faq.q: Does flexitarian diet reduce type 2 diabetes risk?
faq.a: Yes. Research shows a 20% reduction in type 2 diabetes risk for people following flexitarian principles, compared to high-meat diets.

faq.q: How much does flexitarian diet reduce carbon footprint?
faq.a: Approximately 25% lower carbon footprint compared to a meat-heavy omnivorous diet. This contributes meaningfully to personal sustainability goals.

faq.q: Is flexitarian diet good for lowering cholesterol?
faq.a: Yes. Plant-based days are naturally lower in saturated fat, which helps reduce LDL cholesterol. Combined with fish on other days, you get plant fiber plus omega-3s.

sources.name: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
Poore & Nemecek 2018 - Environmental impacts of food production
